title: PostHog Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**PostHog** is an open-source product-analytics + feature-flag + session-replay platform exposing a REST API under `https://app.posthog.com` (or `https://us.posthog.com` / `https://eu.posthog.com` for cloud, or any self-hosted host). This connector — `PostHogConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "posthog"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps every operational surface a Shielva tenant typically needs:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Projects | `/api/projects` | List + read projects |
| Events (capture) | `/capture/` + `/batch/` | Send single + batched events; project_api_key in body |
| Persons | `/api/projects/{id}/persons` | List, get, search, identify, alias users |
| Cohorts | `/api/projects/{id}/cohorts` | List + read cohort definitions |
| Feature Flags | `/api/projects/{id}/feature_flags` | List, get, create flags with filter groups |
| Insights | `/api/projects/{id}/insights` | Saved trends / funnels / retention |
| Dashboards | `/api/projects/{id}/dashboards` | Dashboard listing + detail |
| Actions | `/api/projects/{id}/actions` | Saved action definitions |
| Annotations | `/api/projects/{id}/annotations` | Timeline annotations |
| Experiments | `/api/projects/{id}/experiments` | A/B experiments |
| Query (HogQL) | `/api/projects/{id}/query` | Arbitrary HogQL queries |

The connector normalises persons + events into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff honoring `Retry-After` (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::PostHogHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a Personal API Key

1. Sign in to PostHog (US: <https://us.posthog.com>, EU: <https://eu.posthog.com>, or your self-hosted URL).
2. Click your avatar (bottom-left) → **Personal Settings** → **Personal API Keys**.
3. Click **Create personal API key**. Label it `Shielva connector`.
4. Grant scopes you need — typically `project:read`, `feature_flag:read+write`, `cohort:read`, `insight:read`, `person:read`, `query:read`.
5. Copy the `phx_…` value. **PostHog shows it only once.**

### 2. Find your Project ID and Project API Key

1. From the project picker (top-left), choose the project events should land in.
2. Open **Project Settings** → top of the page shows **Project ID** (numeric, e.g. `12345`) and the **Project API Key** `phc_…`.
3. Copy both.

### 3. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → PostHog**:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `personal_api_key` (secret, required) | `phx_…` from step 1 |
| `project_id` (text, required) | numeric ID from step 2 |
| `project_api_key` (secret, optional) | `phc_…` from step 2 — only needed for capture surfaces |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | default `https://app.posthog.com`; change for EU or self-hosted |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number, optional) | soft cap, default 240 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `personal_api_key` + `project_id` are present (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `PostHogHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`.

### 4. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` next. That probes `GET /api/projects/{project_id}`. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
PostHog uses **two distinct API key types** — the connector wires them independently (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`).

### Credential model

- `personal_api_key` (`phx_…`) — issued from **Personal Settings → Personal API Keys**. Sent as `Authorization: Bearer <personal_api_key>` on every management call. Read/write scope per key.
- `project_api_key` (`phc_…`) — issued from **Project Settings → Project API Key**. **Capture only**. Sent in the JSON body as `api_key`, NEVER as a header. This is the same key shipped in posthog-js client bundles — safe to use from clients.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `personal_api_key` + `project_id`. Initialises `PostHogHTTPClient`. Does **not** call any PostHog endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a stub `TokenInfo` (access_token = personal_api_key). There is no OAuth exchange. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /api/projects/{project_id}` — minimal probe verifying both the personal key AND project access. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None. The keys are persisted in `self.config` via `save_config(...)` at install time. |

### Header contract

Management API request:

```
Authorization: Bearer <personal_api_key>
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
```

Capture API request (`/capture/`, `/batch/`):

```
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
# NO Authorization header
# api_key lives in the JSON body
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `personal_api_key` | secret | yes | `self.personal_api_key` | Authorization Bearer for management API |
| `project_id` | string | yes | `self.project_id` | Default path-segment for `/api/projects/{id}/…`; health probe target |
| `project_api_key` | secret | no | `self.project_api_key` | Body `api_key` for `/capture/` + `/batch/`; required ONLY for capture surfaces |
| `base_url` | string | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://app.posthog.com`) | Override for EU Cloud or self-hosted |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 240) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["personal_api_key", "project_id"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_DEFAULT_BASE = "https://app.posthog.com"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `PostHogConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::PostHogHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `personal_api_key` + `project_id`; initialise `PostHogHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns a stub TokenInfo carrying the personal key.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Probes `GET /api/projects/{project_id}`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates persons + recent events → `NormalizedDocument` → `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Projects
children.content:
### `async list_projects() -> Dict`
`GET /api/projects` — list all projects visible to the personal API key.

### `async get_project(project_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /api/projects/{project_id}`.

children.title: Events / Capture
children.content:
### `async capture_event(distinct_id, event_name, properties=None, timestamp=None) -> Dict`
`POST /capture/` — single event. `project_api_key` is injected into the body.

### `async batch_capture(events) -> Dict`
`POST /batch/` — list of capture dicts. `project_api_key` lives at the top level.

### `async identify_person(distinct_id, properties) -> Dict`
Sends `$identify` event; `properties` merged into `$set` on the person.

### `async alias_distinct_ids(distinct_id, alias) -> Dict`
Sends `$create_alias` event to merge two `distinct_ids` onto one person.

### `async list_events(project_id=None, after=None, before=None, limit=100) -> Dict`
`GET /api/projects/{id}/events` — recent events, optionally bounded by `after`/`before` ISO timestamps.

### `async run_query(query, project_id=None) -> Dict`
`POST /api/projects/{id}/query` — arbitrary HogQL / insight query body: `{query: {...}}`.

children.title: Persons + Cohorts
children.content:
### `async list_persons(project_id=None, search=None, limit=100) -> Dict`
`GET /api/projects/{id}/persons` — optional `search` substring + `limit`.

### `async get_person(person_id, project_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /api/projects/{id}/persons/{person_id}`.

### `async list_cohorts(project_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /api/projects/{id}/cohorts`.

### `async get_cohort(cohort_id, project_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /api/projects/{id}/cohorts/{cohort_id}`.

children.title: Feature Flags
children.content:
### `async list_feature_flags(project_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /api/projects/{id}/feature_flags`.

### `async get_feature_flag(flag_id, project_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /api/projects/{id}/feature_flags/{flag_id}`.

### `async create_feature_flag(key, name, filters=None, active=True, project_id=None) -> Dict`
`POST /api/projects/{id}/feature_flags` — body `{key, name, active, filters}`. If `filters` is omitted the connector injects a default 100%-rollout group.

children.title: Insights · Dashboards · Actions · Annotations · Experiments
children.content:
### `async list_insights(project_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /api/projects/{id}/insights`.

### `async list_dashboards(project_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /api/projects/{id}/dashboards`.

### `async get_dashboard(dashboard_id, project_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /api/projects/{id}/dashboards/{dashboard_id}`.

### `async list_actions(project_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /api/projects/{id}/actions`.

### `async list_annotations(project_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /api/projects/{id}/annotations`.

### `async list_experiments(project_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /api/projects/{id}/experiments`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
PostHogError                          # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── PostHogAuthError                   # 401 / 403
├── PostHogBadRequestError             # 400
├── PostHogNotFoundError               # 404
├── PostHogConflictError               # 409
├── PostHogRateLimitError              # 429 — retry_after_s
└── PostHogServerError                 # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
PostHogNetworkError = PostHogServerError    # legacy name used by older code
PostHogNotFound     = PostHogNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Honor `Retry-After` (seconds). Fall back to exponential backoff `_BACKOFF_BASE * 2 ** attempt`. Retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES = 3`. |
| 5xx | Exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` / `httpx.TransportError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `PostHogNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` maps caught exceptions via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,   TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY,  INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,   CONNECTED)            — logged as warning
network/transport → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, CONNECTED)
other → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `personal_api_key` or `project_id` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. The personal key is shown once at generation — rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 — personal key revoked or wrong host | Verify the key in `Personal Settings → Personal API Keys`. If using EU Cloud, set `base_url=https://eu.posthog.com`. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 — key lacks scope or no access to that project | Edit the key in PostHog and grant the scopes (project, persons, flags, etc.). Confirm the `project_id` belongs to an organisation the key can read. |
| `PostHogError: project_api_key is required to capture events` | `project_api_key` empty in install | Add the `phc_…` key from Project Settings. Capture surfaces (capture_event, batch_capture, identify_person, alias_distinct_ids) need it. |
| `posthog.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (PostHog per-minute cap, default 240) | The client retries with `Retry-After`-aware backoff. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch more aggressively via `batch_capture`. |
| `404 Not Found` on a per-project call | Wrong `project_id` OR project moved to a different organisation | Confirm `project_id` in PostHog → Project Settings. Pass `project_id=...` explicitly to override the install default. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'PostHogNetworkError'` | Older exceptions.py without back-compat aliases | Already fixed — `PostHogNetworkError = PostHogServerError` alias preserved in `exceptions.py`. |
| Events captured but don't show up in PostHog UI | `project_api_key` doesn't match the project you're viewing | The `project_api_key` and the dashboard project must match. Re-check Project Settings → Project API Key. |
